entry.title: Exclude deprecation amount from budget consuming
entry.content:
Hi Experts, currently i have activate cost center budgeting i have noticed in the budget report the deprecation amount consumed from the budget i need to exclude this transaction from budget availability check how can i do this   

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Unable to consume data from a odata service
entry.content:
If you right-click a preview image, you will get base64 value for the image by clicking Copy Image.

If you open the image in a new tab, you will see the base64.

And if you paste that into an image component in Build Apps, the image is displayed.

So I'm asking, as always, to try to narrow down the issue and figure out where the problem is occurring.

entry.title: Condition using a String function
entry.content:
Greetings experts, I am trying to use the endsWith function in as a condition, but I keeping getting an error that says 'type not found for variable "endsWith" '. I have included the Core SDK to use the function.

What I am trying to do is that I am evaluating attachments in Outlook (using the Outlook SDK), and all the attachments that end with .pdf are the attachments that I want to process? But I cannot get the endsWith function to work and I cannot find any documentation that shows an example of this.

SAP Build Process Automation SAP Business Technology Platform

entry.title: Re: Unable to Create Event Consumption Model in On-premise system.
entry.content: can anyone help me with this error please

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP)
entry.content: https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/get-started/jumpstart#setup this link i get from the Getting started with SAP Cloud Application Programming Model learning journey but it gives a 404 error , I dont only require extensions but everything to set up vs code ... thank you also for the extensions sir

entry.title: Is there a limit on number of rules that can be used for 'on save' within a job application?
entry.content: We currently have 40 'on save' rules within 1 job application and this is just for 1 country. We have 30 more countries in the planned roll out and wondering if there is a limit on number of rules and will face issues in the future?

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch / SAP Community Meetup Dortmund 13.02.2025
entry.content:
***English version below***

Der nächste SAP Stammtisch Dortmund findet am 13. Februar 2025 ab 18:30 Uhr statt.

Eingeladen ist jeder, der Lust und Zeit hat, unabhängig vom fachlichen Hintergrund, von Entwickler, Berater, Projekt Manager, Basis Administrator bis hin zum Endanwender. Im Mittelpunkt steht ein lockerer fachlicher Austausch rund um SAP-Themen über Unternehmensgrenzen hinweg.

Wir freuen uns über bekannte und neue Gesichter. Melde dich idealerweise bis zum 05.02. an, damit wir passend planen können. Aber auch eine spontane Teilnahme ist gern gesehen.

Wann? 13.02.2025, 18:30 Uhr
Wo? WENKERS am Markt, Betenstraße 1, 44137 Dortmund

Weitere Infos findest du auch auf unserer Webseite: SAP Stammtisch Dortmund

Bis dann!

---

Our next SAP Community Meetup Dortmund will take place on February 13th 2025, at 06:30 PM.

Everyone is invited, regardless of their professional background, from developers, consultants, project managers, basis administrators to end users. The focus is on a relaxed professional exchange about SAP topics across company boundaries.

We look forward to seeing familiar and new faces. Ideally, please RSVP by the 5th so that we can plan accordingly. However, spontaneous participation is also welcome.

When? February 13th 2025, 6:30 PM
Where? WENKERS am Markt, Betenstraße 1, 44137 Dortmund

You can find additional information on our website (German): SAP Stammtisch Dortmund

See you then!

entry.title: Fixed asset screen layout
entry.content:
Hi Experts, our clint implementing an SAP Public Cloud solution and they require to add some fields for asset master data creation screen for example (Personal number,…) and I did not find any configuration available to modify asset screen layout, please advise what supposed I do to solve this issue.

Thanks in advance

Regards,

Azzam

entry.title: Re: I cannot connect to it (SSH, RDP or SAP GUI)
entry.content: Hi Tsvetinka, Thank you very much for your support! Now I am able to access the system.

entry.title: SAP Job Scheduling Service: How to schedule a job every minute?
entry.content:
You may wonder why the Job Scheduling service has a 5-minute limit on how frequently you can execute schedules. This means that you can execute a schedule every 5 minutes, but not more often than that.

But what if you want to execute your schedule every minute? Is there a way to do that? 🤔

Out of the box, you can't. There are some technical limitations that prevent one schedule from being executed more frequently. However, there is a way to overcome this limitation with a slight workaround. 💡

What is the workaround?

Multiple schedules 🗓️

Imagine you want a job that runs every minute. You need to create 5 schedules with the minimum 5-minute frequency.

Let's say you want the schedule to start at 12:00.

Schedule A : 12:00 (next 12:05)
Schedule B : 12:01 (next 12:06)
Schedule C : 12:02 (next 12:07)
Schedule D : 12:03 (next 12:08)
Schedule E : 12:04 (next 12:09)

And now we are ready, because the next execution at 12:05 will be "Schedule A", 12:06 will be "Schedule B", and so on.

12:00  12:01  12:02  12:03  12:04  12:05  12:06  12:07  12:08  12:09...
  |      |      |      |      |      |      |      |      |      |
  A      B      C      D      E      A      B      C      D      E
 

How to do it in the Job Scheduling service?

Basically, there are two ways to do that - "Repeat Interval" and "Cron" ⏰

Using Repeat Interval

Again, your schedule is executed every 5 minutes, but you have a different start time.

Value: 5 minutes
Start Time: <when you want to start> (Example: Jan 10, 2025, 12:00:00 PM)

 

Every "Start Time" should be increased by 1 minute.

Schedule A : Jan 10, 2025, 12:00:00 PM
Schedule B : Jan 10, 2025, 12:01:00 PM
Schedule C : Jan 10, 2025, 12:02:00 PM
Schedule D : Jan 10, 2025, 12:03:00 PM
Schedule E : Jan 10, 2025, 12:04:00 PM

In the end, you should have something like this:

Using Cron

You need to specify a list of minutes when the schedule is going to be executed. Every schedule should start 1 minute later than the previous one.

 

Schedule A : * * * * * 0,5,10,15,20,25,30,35,40,45,50,55 0
Schedule B : * * * * * 1,6,11,16,21,26,31,36,41,46,51,56 0
Schedule C : * * * * * 2,7,12,17,22,27,32,37,42,47,52,57 0
Schedule D : * * * * * 3,8,13,18,23,28,33,38,43,48,53,58 0
Schedule E : * * * * * 4,9,14,19,24,29,34,39,44,49,54,59 0
 

Execution results

As a result, you can see executions like these in the run logs of each schedule:

Schedule A

Schedule B

entry.title: What's New with SAP Datapshere in Q4, 2024 ✨
entry.content:
💫Join the SAP Datasphere product management team for the session and get the latest information about features and functions delivered in Q4 2024. You can ask the product experts in the Q&A section live during the session and will get an overview on the upcoming features planned for 2024.

The session will cover the following topics:
👉General Product Updates
👉Updates in Data Integration
👉Updates in Data Modeling
👉Updates on various other topics
👉Outlook

We look forward to seeing you!!

📣Speakers:
➡️KP Sauer - Product Management SAP Datasphere
➡️Daniel Ingenhaag - Product Management SAP Datasphere
➡️Florian Neukirch - Product Management SAP Datasphere
➡️Daniel Krämer - Product Management SAP Datasphere
➡️Mostafa Shaarawy - Development SAP Datasphere
➡️Brendon Kellner - Product Management SAP Datasphere
➡️Amogh Kulkarni - Product Management SAP Datasphere

🎬Host:
➡️Tania Dinnendahl - Executive Liaison Manager, SAP Global User Groups Organization

Register for the series and receive updates: https://url.sap/ncal0v

entry.title: Enterprise service Enhancement
entry.content:
Background

There are a few blogs already available showing how to enhance an enterprise service, as well as an SAP guide (SAP Note 2474420 - Enterprise Services Enhancement Guide).  These resources though do not cover usage of the data type after the enhancement.  Hopefully the below blog will help fill in the gap. 

Step By Step Guide

In this example wanted to enhance the below Service Interface

Looking at the message type we can get the data type used

We can enhance this data type to add fields to the main message, equally can enhance sub-nodes if required.

 

In SLD

created product

Create Software component linked to this product

Add Installation Time dependency to component wish to enhance

 

In ESR

Import software component

Once imported can see the dependency set in SLD under the 'Basis Objects'

Create namespace

Add Data Enhancement Type

Nb: worth naming DTE based on data type to be enhanced (ie DTE_TransportationDocumentRequestMessage in this case would be a better name than one used in this example, useful if enhancing multiple nodes)

 

Set the Data Type you with to enhance (selected from the underlying dependency).  This can be a data type at any node level

Add fields that you want in the enhancement

Save and activate

If we now view the message type we are enhancing can see new field added.  Note this only shows under the S4CORE 107 Dependency object in our new Software Component and not in the standard SAP Software component

 

 

In S4 Hana ABAP code in a BADI is used to populate these new fields.

 

When creating an Iflow in NWDS:

Use the original SAP standard service interface

 

Although this is linked to the original unenhanced Message type it is setup as XI compatible, this means that it will accept any message structure (root node remains unchanged so would still work even if not XI compatible)

For operation mapping - again use the original service interface

 

For the message mapping however you need to use the enhancement message type under the newly created software component since this will show the newly added fields.

entry.title: Re: Table Buffering in sm30
entry.content:
Hi @karthik_sub,

Use AL12 to check the table buffering.

First check if the records are buffered using the following options. For example, I could get T005 buffer to be filled using the tcode for House Banks.

If you need to reset the buffer for a table to be sure, you can use the following option from AL12:

Alex

entry.title: Re: FBL*H transactions - OOM Dump(STATEMENT_MEMORY_LIMIT_FROM_GLOBAL_CONFIG) and analysis
entry.content: Try to execute an SQL trace for FLB3H to get actual SQL statement that triggered the dump.

entry.title: Re: Fixed asset screen layout
entry.content: Please, add a tag such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance

entry.title: Re: Unable to see the Joule entitlement in BTP
entry.content:
Hey @imkanabbas93,

the Joule Entitlement is provisioned when you signed up for the Joule Embedded SKU. You can get in touch with your SAP Account Executive to get it settled. Make sure to ask him to provide the Joule Embedded Entitlement on the BTP Global Account of choice!

Best Regards,

Felix

entry.title: Re: How to configure IP based access to SAP Sales and Service Version 2
entry.content:
Hello @SunkariPraveen,

Thank you very much for this detailed blog and this will be very helpful to configure IP based access to SAP Sales and Service Version 2.

Best Regards,
Harish

entry.title: Re: Fixed asset screen layout
entry.content:
Look for 'Field Extensibility' in Asset Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition 2302

(Look for Asset master data in documentation, I only find this blog)

entry.title: Re: Enquiry about Joule activation using BTP Trail account
entry.content:
Hey @karthik1gobburu,

as of January 2025 this is currently not possible. You need the Joule Embedded Entitlement that you get with signing up for the SAP Joule Embedded SKU. You can get this 0$ SKU by talking to your assigned SAP Account Executive. Then only you can setup Joule but only on your general BTP Global Account. Trial is out-of-scope right now.

Best Regards,

Felix

entry.title: Re: S2S VPN requirement?
entry.content: Thank you Tomasz.

entry.title: Re: Joule with S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hey @ebsor,

to get started with SAP Joule in S/4HANA Public Cloud, you need to sign up for the 0$ Joule Embedded SKU. You can talk to your SAP Account Executive (or Partner Contact) about that. I can recommend this blog for the further setup routine.

Best Regards,

Felix

entry.title: Re: S2S VPN requirement?
entry.content: Your assumption is correct in that the target environment is RISE. They are an installed base client on ECC. They will retire the old once the new is up and running.

entry.title: Re: S2S VPN requirement?
entry.content: Their ultimate question is on users connecting from home or mobile into the RISE environment. They do not want their users to have to pop up a VPN to get into RISE. Any thoughts you can share on this?

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule with SAP BTP Trail Version
entry.content:
Hey @karthik1gobburu,

as of January 2025 this is currently not possible. You need the Joule Embedded Entitlement that you get with signing up for the SAP Joule Embedded SKU. You can get this 0$ SKU by talking to your assigned SAP Account Executive. Then only you can setup Joule but only on your general BTP Global Account. Trial is out-of-scope right now.

Best Regards,

Felix

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP)
entry.content:
@Bucule @WouterLemaire 

Install the below Extensions (SAP BAS and Remote Access) which will connect to your SAP Business Application Studio (BAS)

https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=SAPOSS.app-studio-toolkit

https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=SAPOSS.app-studio-remote-access

entry.title: Step-by-Step Guide to ECC to S/4HANA Migration with Migration Cockpit
entry.content:
Migration Project Overview: ECC to S/4HANA

The migration project from ECC to S/4HANA is divided into three key phases, each with distinct roles and responsibilities:

1. Pre-Migration

This phase lays the groundwork for a smooth migration.

  • Key Prerequisite: A BASIS consultant must apply SAP notes to the ECC server. This is essential to initiate the pre-migration process.

  • Primary Activities: Functional consultants play a crucial role in preparing the system for migration.

2. Technical Migration

This phase involves converting the data and the system to S/4HANA.

  • Responsible Teams: BASIS consultants and ABAP developers (ABAPers) are responsible for executing technical migration tasks.

  • Key Notes: All data will be migrated to S/4HANA, except for Rebate Agreement data.

3. Post-Migration

This phase ensures that all configurations and critical functionalities are operational.

  • Responsible Teams: Functional consultants are primarily responsible for post-migration tasks.

  • Primary Activities:

    • BP Configuration: Set up Business Partner roles and data.
    • Migrate Condition Master & Credit Master Data: Ensure all pricing conditions and credit limits are transferred.
    • Configure Credit Management: Update and align credit management settings with S/4HANA capabilities.
Optional Post-Migration Activities
  • Settlement Management Configuration: Align with S/4HANA's streamlined processes.
  • BRF+ (Business Rules Framework Plus): Configure advanced rules and workflows for decision automation.

Pre-Migration Configuration & Activities (In ECC)

To initiate the migration project, the BASIS consultant must apply the necessary SAP notes in the ECC system. Once this is done, the following configuration and activities must be completed:

  1. BASIS has to apply SAP notes, so we start with below configuration
  2. CVI configuration ( Customer Vendor Integration)
  3. Define BP roles
  4. Define BP groupings
  5. Define BP number ranges
  6. Assign account groups to BP roles
  7. Assign account groups to BP groupings
  8. Upgradation check report generate (this report ensures system readiness to migrate customers and vendors to the BP framework)

         Execution Details:

  • Transaction Code: SA38 or SE38
  • Program Name: PRECHEK_UPGRADATION_REPORT
  • Requirement: The report must complete successfully without errors to proceed with customer and vendor migration.

    9. Simplification Item Check Report (this report identifies errors or inconsistencies across all modules before proceeding with migration)

         Execution Details:

  • Program Name: /SDF/RC_START_CHECK
  • Error Code Guidance:
    • Return Code < 7: Errors can be skipped; migration activities (pre & post) can proceed.
    • Return Code = 7: Pre-migration can proceed, but issues will block post-migration activities.
    • Return Code > 7: Stop migration entirely and resolve errors before continuing.

10. Migration of Customers and vendors to Business Partners (BP) (all customers and vendors in the ECC server are migrated to the BP framework using the SAP Migration Cockpit)

Technical Migration: ECC to S/4HANA

The technical migration phase involves converting the ECC system to S/4HANA. This critical process is managed by ABAP and BASIS consultants, and it requires the client to halt business operations on the Production server during migration.

Key Activities in Technical Migration

  1. System Downtime

    • Business Impact: All business activities must be paused during this phase.
    • Duration: Typically spans 3 to 4 days, depending on the system size and complexity.

  2. Data Conversion

    • Scope of Conversion:
      • Master Data: Includes customer, vendor, material, and other static data.
      • Transactional Data: Sales orders, purchase orders, and other ongoing business transactions.
      • Enhancements & Reports: Custom ABAP programs, reports, and user enhancements are adjusted for compatibility with S/4HANA.
      • Custom Tables (Z Tables): Migrated to S/4HANA environment.
      • Conditions: Migrated partially; the rest is addressed in post-migration.
      • Rebate Agreement Master Data: Not converted during technical migration 

  3. Condition Master Data Handling

    • After the technical migration, the Condition Master Data needs additional processing.
    • Reason: Data is updated in the ECC KONV table, but in S/4HANA, it must be migrated to the PRCD_ELEMENTS table.
    • Resolution:
      • Technical consultants apply relevant SAP Notes to redirect data to the correct tables (e.g., VAUK → VBAK, VUBP → VBAP).

  4. Post-Migration Handover

    • Once the technical migration is successfully completed, the system is handed over to functional consultants for post-migration activities.

Post-Migration Activities (S/4HANA System)

After the completion of the technical migration, the S/4HANA system will be operational. The functional consultant is responsible for ensuring that all configurations and data migrations are completed to enable seamless business operations in the new system.

Core Post-Migration Activities:

  1. BP Configuration (Direction: BP → Customer)

    • Align Business Partner (BP) data to ensure it maps correctly to customer data in the S/4HANA system.
    • Validate the consistency of BP roles, groupings, and mappings post-migration.

  2. Migrate Condition Master

    • Transfer pricing and other condition records from the KONV table (ECC) to the PRCD_ELEMENTS table (S/4HANA).

  3. Configure Credit Master

    • Set up credit management settings as per S/4HANA’s new credit management functionalities.
    • Map and validate credit limits for customers.

  4. Migrate Credit Master

    • Transfer credit-related master data to the new system.
    • Ensure proper linking of credit data with Business Partner records.

Optional Configurations (Customer-Specific)

  • Configure Settlement Management

    • Set up the new Settlement Management framework to replace traditional rebate agreement functionality.

  • Configure BRF+ (Business Rules Framework Plus)

    • Implement BRF+ for generating and automating billing document outputs.
    • Configure rules for customized outputs as per the business requirements.

  • Advanced Available-to-Promise (AATP)

    • Configure and activate the AATP functionality for enhanced order fulfillment capabilities, including backorders and product allocation.

 

Migration Process with Existing Landscape: Converting from ECC to S/4HANA

When migrating from ECC to S/4HANA with the existing landscape, a structured approach to ensure a smooth transition. Below are the key steps to follow during the migration process:

  1. Pre-Migration Preparation

    • Close and move any pending TRs (Transport Requests) that are near completion.
    • For TRs that can't be closed soon, inform BASIS consultants, who will back up and apply the TRs during "Hanatization."

  2. Backup

    • Take a backup of the Production system to Development.

  3. SAP Notes Application

    • BASIS consultants apply necessary SAP Notes for the migration process.

Prerequisite for Migration Project

After BASIS applies SAP notes, the following steps are needed to initiate migration:

Step 1: Activate Post Processing Orders:

Go to SPRO → Cross Application Component → General Application Functions → Post Processing Office → Business Process → Activate Creation of Post Processing Orders.

In ECC, the approach is Customer to BP. In S/4HANA, it will be BP to Customer, so this needs to be activated.

Optional in ECC, Required in S/4HANA:

  • CVI_03 – BP to Customer
  • CVI_04 – BP to Vendor

 

Step 2: Activate Function Modules

Go to SPRO → Cross Application Component → SAP Business Partner → Data Distribution → Activate Function Modules

Check Function Modules Activation:
Ensure the following function modules are activated:

  • COM_BUPA_MWX_CREATE_MAIN
  • COM_BUPA_MWX_CREATE_REL
  • BUPA_CREATE_CHANGE_POINTER
  • BUPA_OUTBOUND_ALE_REL
  • MDS_BUPA_OUTBOUND

These modules are essential for proper Business Partner data handling and distribution during migration.

Step 3: Synchronization Control for Master Data

Go to SPRO → Cross Application Components → SAP Business Partner → Master Data Synchronization → Synchronization Control → Synchronization Object.

Step 4: Activate PPO Request for Platform Objects

Go to SPRO → Cross Application Components → SAP Business Partner → Master Data Synchronization → Synchronization Control → Synchronization Control.

activate the PPO request for platform objects in the dialogue. This is essential for the handling of post-processing of Business Partner data during the migration process.

 

Step 5: Activate Synchronization Options for ECC to S/4HANA Migration

Go to SPRO → Cross Application Components → SAP Business Partner → Master Data Synchronization → Synchronization Control → Synchronization Control.

Activating these synchronization options ensures the correct mapping and transfer of customer and vendor data to the Business Partner (BP) model in S/4HANA.

Step 6: Activate Assignment of Contact Person for Customer Integration

Go to SPRO → Cross Application Components → SAP Business Partner → Master Data Synchronization → Customer-Vendor Integration → BP Settings → Setting for Customer Integration.

This ensures that contact person data is properly linked and integrated during the migration from ECC to S/4HANA.

 

Steps for Customer to Business Partner Migration

  1. Find Customer Account Group & Entries:

    • Access the KNA1 table to identify the Customer Account Group.
    • Count the number of customer entries in the table.

  2. Document Account Group Details:

    • Record the Customer Account Assignment Group, Number Intervals, From and To Number Range, and External Indicator.

  3. Configure BP Settings:

    • In S/4HANA, configure the BP Account Assignment Group, BP Number Range, From and To Number Range, and External Indicator to align with the customer settings.

  4. Post-Migration Comparison:

    • After migration, compare the number of entries in KNA1 with BUT000 (Business Partner Master) and VI_LINK tables to ensure data consistency.

These steps ensure the proper migration and synchronization of customer data to the Business Partner model in S/4HANA.

Before diving into the migration process, lets understand the migration scenarios and the Business Partner (BP) approach.

3 Migration Scenarios for BP Data Consistency

Scenario 1 – Both Tables Updated (Status: OK):
If both BUT000 (Business Partner) and CVI_LINK (Customer-Vendor Integration) tables are updated, the status is considered OK, indicating successful migration.

Scenario 2 – BUT000 Updated, CVI_LINK Not Updated (Status: OK):
If only BUT000 is updated but CVI_LINK is not, the status is still considered OK. In this case, run the report ZCUSTOMER_LINK_CHECK_REPORT to ensure the issue is tracked and resolved.

Scenario 3 – BUT000 Not Updated (Status: NOT OK):
If BUT000 is not updated, regardless of the CVI_LINK update status, the migration status is NOT OK. This requires raising an issue with SAP support for resolution.

These scenarios help in quickly identifying and resolving migration issues for customer and BP data synchronization.

BP APPROACH

1. BP = CUSTOMER                                                3. BP # CUSTOMER

     BP ≠ VENDOR                                                        BP ≠  VENDOR

 

2. BP ≠  CUSTOMER                                                  4. BP = CUSTOMER   ( not in use)

    BP = VENDOR                                                           BP = VENDOR

 

BP Table- BUT000

BP- Customer Link Information –  CVI_CUST_LINK

LINK FIELD BETWEEN BUT000 & CVI_CUST_LINK IS “PARTNER_GUID” i.e. Business partner GUID.

 

Step 1: Extract Account Groups from KNA1 Table for Migration

 To get the account groups that are actively in use (not all available account groups), follow these steps once Activation is done.

Go to SE16 → KNA1 → execute → Select Account group → Filter → F4 → get all the account group → Paste in Excel

For Account group number of entries (customers ) are 4 in the table.

Step 2: Document in Excel

CAG- Customer account group; NI- Number range interval

Now we need to create BP configurations to fill up the excel for BP information as shown.

Step 3: Configure BP in ECC System

SPRO → Cross application components → SAP Business Partner → Basic Setting  Business Partner Roles → Define BP roles 

  • Define groupings and number assignment

BP grouping ZD01 created for Customer account group- JD02.

For accounted account group JD02 we have 4 entries. As of now BP has not been created so no entry in table- BUT000.

 

Execute Upgradation Check Report for Customer BP and CVI Configuration

To check for errors related to Customer BP Configuration or CVI Configuration, follow these steps:

  1. Execute the Upgradation Check Report:

    • Go to Transaction SE38 or SA38 (ABAP Program Execution).
    • Enter the program name: /SDF/RC_START_CHECK (for the Upgradation Check Report).

  2. Run the Report:

    • Execute the report. This will perform checks across various configurations and highlight any issues related to Customer to BP migration or CVI configuration.

  3. Review the Errors:

    • The report will display errors and inconsistencies that need to be resolved before migration. Pay particular attention to issues with Customer to BP mapping and CVI Configuration.

We are getting some error related to Customer BP configuration or CVI configuration.

Analyze the Error:

Trying to execute Simplification Item Check report.

Report shows an error indicating that the BP role direction needs to be configured from Customer to BP.

Follow these steps to resolve it:

SPRO → Cross Application Components → Master data synchronization → Customer-Vendor Integration → Business Partner settings → Settings for Customer Integration → Define BP role for direction Customer to BP

Assign account group to BP group.

SPRO →  Cross Application Components → Master data synchronization → Customer-Vendor Integration → Business Partner settings → Settings for Customer Integration → field assignment for customer integration → assign keys → Define number assignment for direction Customer to BP

Re-run the check report, and if no issues are found, proceed with the migration steps.

Steps to Migrate Customer to Business Partner in Migration Cockpit

Access Migration Cockpit:

  • Go to Migration Cockpit (Transaction: MDS_LOAD_COCKPIT).

Select “customer – Business Partner” combination → right  side → mention account group in “field name”, I in “Include”, EQ in “Option” account group in “Lower limit” → Execute

Click on “Monitor”  Queue → Register & activate queue → refresh it → get the status

Check the BUT000 table for BP group ZD01 and verify if there are 4 entries, matching the number of entries in KNA1.

entry.title: SAP successfactors transport management with cloud transport management
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Need your advice.

Does SAP provide functionality to use cloud transport management to move transports to test and prod landscapes in SAP successfactors. We would like to use a central transport strategy for all the cloud products but could not find anything for SAP successfactors transport management.

Thank you!

Regards,

Debanshu Meher

entry.title: Re: CIS (Cloud Identity Services) - IAS & IPS
entry.content:
@VedettevanKleef 

Once you have SAP CIS (Cloud identity Services) you will have both IAS and IPS.  

To depends on your use case for using IAS and IPS.  

IPS is mainly used for syncing users from one environment to another environment with providing approriate access to the application(READ; WRITE) with assigning groups

https://github.com/yogananda-muthaiah/SAP-Sucessfactors-Incentive-Management/tree/main/Integrations/IPS 

IAS is mainly used for storing all users with approrirate groups and enabling Single Sign on for different SAP and Non SAP applications for those users. similarly to IAM solutions which is there in market.
https://github.com/yogananda-muthaiah/SAP-Sucessfactors-Incentive-Management/tree/main/Integrations/IAS

entry.title: Re: SAP CAL S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, Fully-Activated Appliance
entry.content: Hi, one more question - is there a way to set this up as a separate windows app server and linux db server instead of a central instance on linux? I can't seem to find any information on whether this is possible or not. Cheers, James

entry.title: How to pass data Smart Forms TO Adobe Forms Conversion Using CL_HRFORM_HRF02?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Does anyone know how to convert a payroll from HR Forms CL_HRFORM_HRF02 to Adobe Forms?

I've already done the Adobe part, but I can't get the data that already exists in HR FORMS to the new form.

I'm having trouble with the CL_HRFORM_HRF02 class because I need the "FDATA_SAVE" attribute via debug, but it's private and I couldn't find any "get" or related method that could pull it.

I'm reusing the Z program trying to change only the call with the correct data passing (scr2.png).

The closest I could get was using the system memory option (scr1.png), but I still couldn't get there.

Could someone please help me?

entry.title: Re: Basic Credit Management (BD6): Exlcude a payment term (prepayments) from credit limit calculatio
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
 
I believe I have partially solved the problem by using a billing plan with a 100% downpayment. As a result, the sales order no longer counts against the credit limit.
 
However, this has opened another issue for us:
The incoming downpayment seems to reduce the customer's credit exposure, regardless if the related sales order was a prepayment sales order or not.
 

For example:
Let's assume a customer has a credit limit of €200,000 and we create two sales orders for this customer.

1. Sales Order 1001: A regular sales order valued at €100,000 with NT30 (30 days net) payment terms. This utilizes 50% (100.000/200.000) of the credit limit as expected.


2. Sales Order 1002: A sales order valued at €100,000 with a 100% downpayment request billing plan. This is initially ignored by credit management.

 

 


However, when an invoice from sales order 1002 for the downpayment is created and the payment is received, the customer's credit exposure is reduced temporary to €0. Only after the delivery and the final invoice from sale order 1002 is created does the credit exposure increase back to the correct value of €100,000 (from NT30 sales order 1001).
 
This is problematic for us because, during the period between the payment and the delivery, it appears that the customer has €0 credit exposure. In our case, there can be a significant time gap between these two points, making it difficult to maintain an accurate overview of the actual credit exposure at any given time.
 
I was hoping that the incoming downpayment, marked as a prepayment, would not alter the existing current exposure of the customer.
 
Do you have any recommendations on how this issue can be avoided?
 
Thanks in advance
Simon

entry.title: Simplify FP&A with Multiple Hierarchies in the Standard Account Dimension of SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

Financial planning often spans multiple business areas, such as Finance, Sales, Workforce, and Capital. To manage this complexity, SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) introduced multiple hierarchies in the Standard Account Dimension, offering unparalleled reporting, forecasting, and data entry flexibility.

With the Rapid FP&A Suite for SAP Analytics Cloud, we’ve taken this capability to the next level, showcasing best practices. In this blog, I’ll explore how these hierarchies work within the Rapid FP&A Suite, explain their technical setup, and provide resources to help you get started.

Understanding the Value of Multiple Hierarchies

Multiple hierarchies in SAP Analytics Cloud allow you to:

  • Group accounts differently for specific business purposes.
  • Simultaneously manage current budgets and forecasts for future scenarios.
  • Streamline workflows across models while ensuring consistency in data.

For example, the Standard Account Dimension in the Rapid FP&A Suite includes four hierarchies, each tailored to a specific business need:

1. Finance (H1)

  • Integrated with SAP S/4HANA P&L and Balance Sheet accounts.
  • Enriched in SAP Analytics Cloud by structures for Cash Flow, Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), Driver Input accounts, and Staging accounts.
  • Serves as the foundation for the other hierarchies.

2. Sales (H2)

  • Leverages Gross Margin accounts from H1.
  • Adds a Volume account and introduces its own KPIs, Driver Input accounts, and Staging accounts.

3. Workforce (H3)

  • Draws from Employee Expense accounts in H1.
  • Includes a Headcount (FTE) structure, Workforce KPIs, Driver Input accounts, and Staging accounts.

4. Capital (H4) - release date is February 15, 2025

  • Built on Gross Asset and Depreciation accounts from H1.
  • Expands with Capital-specific KPIs, Driver Input accounts, and staging accounts.

How the Rapid FP&A Suite Demonstrates Best Practices

The Rapid FP&A Suite is designed to showcase these hierarchies in a practical, hands-on environment. Customers can Download the suite for no charge and explore:

  • Pre-built models for Finance, Sales, Workforce, and Capital.
  • Hierarchies that demonstrate data consistency and alignment across different planning models.
  • A flexible structure that reflects real-world use cases using sample data

The suite illustrates the value of multiple hierarchies and offers a downloadable, working solution to help organizations adapt these practices to their needs.

How do you Download the free Rapid FP&A Suite?
Downloading the Rapid FP&A Suite is straightforward and hassle-free.
Follow these steps to get started:
1. Log in to your SAP Analytics Cloud account.   Please don't use a production tenant.
2. Navigate to the "Content Network" on the left-hand side panel of SAC
3. Select "Business Content"
4. Search for "Rapid" in the upper right-hand 'Search' box - you should see Rapid Finance, Workforce, Sales
4. Follow the instructions to complete the download and installation of each of the Rapid modules (Rapid Finance, Rapid Sales, Rapid Workforce, Rapid Capital).

Resources to Get Started

Here’s how you can dive deeper into multiple hierarchies and the Rapid FP&A Suite:

  • Help Documentation: Learn more about setting up and managing multiple hierarchies in the Standard Account Dimension at this SAP link on Help.sap.com
  • Rapid FP&A WorkZone: Join our Rapid FP&A WorkZone to access enablement materials, presentations, and collaboration opportunities.
  • Rapid FP&A Blog: Explore the foundational blog post for Rapid FP&A on SAP Community.
  • Follow Me on LinkedIn: Connect with me, Mark P. Burke, to stay updated on FP&A Best Practices and SAP-related insights.
  • Join the Rapid FP&A Group on LinkedIn: Engage with other practitioners and discuss best practices in the Rapid FP&A private group.

Conclusion

Multiple hierarchies in the Standard Account Dimension of SAP Analytics Cloud offer a powerful way to manage financial planning across business areas. By leveraging these hierarchies, such as within the Rapid FP&A Suite, organizations can streamline their workflows and unlock new levels of flexibility and precision.

If you haven’t explored the Rapid FP&A Suite yet, I encourage you to download it, join the WorkZone, and see how it simplifies financial planning and analysis.

I look forward to your thoughts and feedback. Let’s connect and continue the conversation—whether here or on LinkedIn!

SAP Analytics Cloud SAP Analytics Cloud for planning SAP S/4HANA SAP S/4HANA Cloud, extended edition

entry.title: Re: Condition using a String function
entry.content:
Hi, It sounds like the issue you’re encountering is due to incorrect syntax. Instead of using endsWith directly, you should try using it as a method on the attachment string. Here’s how you can do it: Step6.attach.endsWith(".pdf").

 

Martina

entry.title: Integrate Custom App Into SAP Fiori Launchpad Issue
entry.content:
Hi all,
I´m trying to publish the ABAP RAP created but when I perform the step to publish the app to the page/space created into S4HC public cloud I´m not able to see it:

It says "Please check if the spaces are assigned to a role with catalogs" but it is already assigned:

The IAM app was created, the custom business catalog has the IAM app assigned to it, the custom business role has the custom business catalog assigned to it but when I try to browse the BC into App Finder I can´t see it.

The BC was created and published because I can see it using Maintain Business Catalog app and I can see it from Eclipse IDE too.

Any tips please?
Links I have used:
Integrate List Report Into SAP Fiori Launchpad | SAP Tutorials
Develop an SAP Fiori Application UI | SAP Help Portal
Add Your App to SAP Fiori Launchpad | SAP Help Portal

Thanks in advance,
Mauricio Predolim

Thanks.

entry.title: SAP TM Transaction WUF-Where-Used List
entry.content:
SAP TM Transaction WUF-Where-Used List

The Transaction WUF - Where-Used List. is part of the process for the Archiving for Master Data Objects which use the transaction WUF_OBJCUST to review the objects that the Where-Used Framework (WUF) includes in its where-used check.

You can use transaction WUF to detect and display dependencies. The system checks if the object which is checked is used by an instance of the defined sub-objects.

The WUF is used during archiving. If it does not find a usage, the object will be archived when the residence period is reached.

Also, the transaction WUF allow to search by Master Data to find where the objects are used, i.e Check where the Calculation Sheet is assigned to which freight Agreement.

On the SAP Easy Access screen, choose Logistics   Transportation Management  Master Data  WUF – Where-Used List.

On the Where-Used List screen, enter the object type you want to check. In the Selection Criteria screen area, enter the object value you want to check.  

For example, enter a Rate Table number, You can also search using F4.

 

Under Object Name on the left-hand side of the screen, select the object you want to check. On the right-hand side of the screen, choose the appropriate hosting object. For example, choose Calculation Sheet.

The system displays the where-used information for the hosting object you select.

For example, if we search by Location SP_XXXX we can see where the location is used by different Objects mark in red (Schedule- Settlement Documents- Resources – Transportation Lane, etc).

Using the WUF to Check Where an Object is Used | SAP Help Portal

2419704 - Cannot Mark Business Partner for Deletion or Archiving in SAP TM - SAP for Me

Schedule | SAP Help Portal

Hope this help you!

Thank you and regards.

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: Datasphere Transport management is terrible, what am I missing?
entry.content:
The longer I am using Datasphere, the more frustrated I become about some design choices and at this point I am wondering if I miss something.

I have a very simple landscape with a dev Datasphera and a prod Datasphere. Most of the time, I want to transport medium sized changes to already existing models and views.

Currently, you can either use packages, where you always have to grab all dependencies but you do have the option to organize all changes into some sort of "unit" or "package" or you do it manually and you might forget to transport a change you made.

So exactly the option that I need the most: Package a few changes into a transport without the entire dependency chain, is missing.

All I need is the ability to create a package without all the dependencies. Very simple! And that is how the transport management works in SAP R3 and R4? What was the reasoning not to do it with Datasphere?

On top of that, the UI is especially slow there. Simply opening the initial dialogue takes 30 seconds.

What it all boils down to is that even minor changes that should be 5 minutes take me 30 minutes because of the clunky and slow implementation. 

But I cannot rule out, that I do something wrong so I am here posing the question if other people see it the same way or if make mistakes.

entry.title: Re: Can old BW 731 system consume data from EC Payroll in...
entry.content:
You have to create the datasources on BW side individually pointing to the corresponding ODP-s:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_NETWEAVER_731_BW_ABAP/ccc9cdbdc6cd4eceaf1e5485b1bf8f4b/41752b565b3642d68b50d9c518748e89.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Are you ready for asset management capabilities of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Configuring SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Asset Management learning journey.

Overview
This Learning Journey provides comprehensive knowledge on implementation and customizing of technical objects, preventive maintenance and maintenance operations. It is designed to provide consultants with practical skills and solid detail knowledge to execute all required customizing settings in the areas of SAP S/4HANA Asset Management. In addition, consultants will learn about specialized processes and functions.

Learning objectives
Acquire detailed knowledge on how to effectively manage, structure and customize technical objects.

  • Learn and apply preventive maintenance processes, including the management of task lists and the creation and scheduling of various types of maintenance plans.
  • Gain proficiency in customizing settings with a key focus on maintenance notifications, maintenance orders, order costing and order settlement.

Prerequisites

  • Applying SAP S/4HANA Asset Management

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

check the KBA:

2736437 Error: RSDSO_ROLLBACK153 "Deletion of overlapping requests not supported for DSO (advanced) <ADSO Name>"

entry.title: Re: SAP HRM structure and configuration

Dear Bushkam,

Before you are creating a new employee file, you need first to create the position in organization structure through PPOME.

Then you can add the same position id in personal action, when you enter the position ID, organization unit and other details will come automatically.

regards

ALI

entry.title: SYBASE_SAM_CAPACITY for a Nutanix AHV
entry.content:
I need to obtain the SYBASE_SAM_CAPACITY for a Nutanix AHV server to generate the sybase license for a VM that was created in that SYBASE_SAM_CAPACITY.

When I tried to obtain it the value of SYBASE_SAM_CAPACITY changed in each execution of the sysamcap script.

entry.title: Mass/Material Balance on Continuous I/O through SF and Finished products on SCM PPDS
entry.content:
Hi Gurus, I am looking for some help regarding the material flow balance between SF process and packing. I am using the continuous I/O functionality, maintaining distribution key (GLEI) on BOM and PDS, also i am maintaining Pegging with continuous I/O with min passing amount of 1. Im running PP/DS optimizer considering continuous flow.
I am in a massive food industry and the rule we are following is that SemiFinished process runs at 100% all time at max rate.

We need to search for an optimized schedule that maintains all SF production in zero (or near), while I schedule my Finished products on packing area. The thing is that I need to restrict some behaviours on PPDS optimizer because sometimes the optimizer considers that (shift over shift) my packing lines can consume higher amount of SF because the previous shift has left some stock there, and it is not correct because the process line is continuous, we cannot hold semifinished products to use it after some time. 

I am sharing some screens on what i've done and hope you could help me.

The last line is SF product stock, some times the consumption is more than the production, so stocks levels decrease. I need to find a way that this do not happens.

 

 

Thanks, 

 

Francisco

entry.title: Re: Rental condition type should auto calculate the unit...
entry.content:
Hi Karthik,

indeed that is possible with the "Manage Contract" app, but also with the new "Contract Management" app.

Prerequisite is that you additionally create master data objects representing the Wind Turbine. This object is called either Usable Object in a lease-in scenario or Rentable Object in a lease-out scenario (in both cases without portfolio structure). How to create those master data objects is described in the Master data script (MDS) in https://help.sap.com/docs/s4hana-best-practices/7ad/create-usable-object-without-portfolio (7AD). To capture the MW capacity within those objects please use the measurement section, for which you need to configure a dedicated measurement type in the configuration. By having that in plase you can maintain the MW capacity time-dependently in those objects.

In the contract you then have to assign this Usable or Rentable Object (depending on the scenario) and pick the calculation formula "Object Measurement" in the condition. Here you have then to use the used measurement type from above and enter the 6 as Unit Price. Then the system automatically calculates the amount for the condition.

Hopefully that helps.

Kind regards

Nils

entry.title: Re: I am having trouble accessing my study material in assigned Learning Journey via imbedded links.
entry.content:
Hi @AnneGR 
Can you please share a sample from where you start to open the material?

Margit

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
@dduron 

Hello,

Can confirm we experienced the same issue. It only restored to default the text for the TFG associated with the Box 14, mapping of wage types was not affected. To correct this, we configured back the custom text.

Regards

entry.title: Re: TMS - Transport SAP Cloud Integration (CI/CPI) Content with Transport Management Service (TMS) a
entry.content:
@TiaXu Many thanks for writing this blog, it is great to see how this has helped a lot of customers who are trying to get started with integration content transport.
I am the service owner of Content Agent service and want to share a correction with the set up you have mentioned in your blog. You don't need a specific CAS subaccount. Instead all configuration done in CAS subaccount should be done in the Source (DEV) subaccount. 
Destination 1 -> pointing to CAS service instance (standard plan) also subscribed in Source (DEV) account.
Destination 2 -> pointing to Cloud Integration service (it-rt, api plan) also subscribed in Source (DEV) account
Destination 3 -> pointing to Cloud Transport Management service instance subscribed in Central account. 

Below is a diagram for your reference. Would appreciate a lot if you could please make the suggested changes.

entry.title: Cannot see community.sap.com attached graphs
entry.content:
Good afternoon,

I am trying to see the entire content of the following blog:

https://community.sap.com/t5/additional-blogs-by-members/same-day-ach-how-to-set-up-your-sap-system-1-of-2/ba-p/13133409#:~:text=Same%2DDay%20ACH%20lessens%20outstanding,three%20settlement%20windows%20each%20day.

but only the text is shown, when I try to display/download the graphs, I receive the following error:

403. That’s an error.

Your client does not have permission to get URL /gsBIXI-VD6YqHD_U-DayhzGfP7YDwbuYgPGxp5w34Car4pbCmujKSSZFz7hN8A9XxfMp-xsrm94oVDMJhNeLT8zY-30tpWc7DJEr099My2OfObsxUVXoZ9xiByAybiUW3NoWcqM from this server. (Client IP address: 147.161.190.118)

Please help.

Regards, Armando

entry.title: Re: How to read/save AL11 file in ABAP Cloud
entry.content: In ECC there was a very generic requirement to save the data in application server then access later. If we want to replicate same in Cloud then how to achieve it?

entry.title: Re: Delimiting IT2001 with begda before earliest retroacitve date
entry.content: SAP released a note 3197866 - Partial Delete for Time Management Infotypes, explaining that delimiting is not an options, but partial deleting is what should be used. This is still not working for our company when you are trying to end the absence after the last working day. The system tells you that the record is being deleted on a non-working day. When you adjust the date, it puts you back in the same situation of retro'ing back to the beginning of the record you are trying to delimit.

entry.title: Re: Facing an error at cf push in step9 deploy and run ap...
entry.content: I faced this error and resolved it by building the project before running the command cf push --vars-file . It looks like cf push command looks for vars.file only in the output directory of your project build . If your project is built successfully before running this command, I think this error should be resolved.

entry.title: Re: Strange behavior in Decision step for WORKING_DAY in...
entry.content:
This is really really old. But since I think the issue still exists, I want to suggest a possible workaround.
As mentioned by others the formula (like fm DATE_CONVERT_TO_FACTORYDATE) shifts a date that is not a working date either to the following (+) or the previous working date (-) depending on "correct option". So you cannot know whether it is really a working day or not. Obviously this is really strange and very misleading. Solution could be to add a check if you get the same result for correct option "+" and "-". If you get the same result, it is a date that has not been shifted, so it is a geniune working day. Attached a screenshot fot the 7th working day. Hope this can be useful. Best regards

entry.title: Re: Change company code from XYZ to ABC
entry.content: Basically we have company code XYZ and we want to replace it with ABC and block the company code XYZ. We completely want to replace the company code not the name. Yes we do have the master data in the company code.

entry.title: Re: GRC Process Control - Add a question to a survey
entry.content:
Hi ,

You will not be able to do it as survey already scheduled . You will be creating data inconsistency by adding question mid way.

You can create new survey or complete this one before adding new question.

Regards,

Uppdeep

entry.title: Start your SAP Solutions for Quote to Cash Management Learning Journey today!
entry.content:
Get started on boosting your skills today, without spending a dime! You can find all of the following resources at learning.sap.com.

 

Design Principles:
All these free learning journeys include valuable learning content using different kinds of media, such as videos, graphics, animations, system demos, quizzes, etc.. These are the characteristics of the new SAP Solutions for Quote to Cash Management learning journeys:

  • Designed primarily for self-paced learning
  • Tailored setup to fit real learners’ needs
  • Composed for efficient use of our learner’s time

Learning Platform - One-Stop-Shop for all delivery formats
Through different clickable tabs, the learning journey gives you directions on related deliverables (with cost).

  • Passing the quizzes at the end of each unit will lead to a (free) Record of Achievement unless the learning journey is part of a certification track. 
  • The Hands-on Practice area offers you a link to booking options to get access to our training system environment. Most of the learning journeys listed above go along with a hands-on training system. Exercises are designed for any deployment version. As before, learners can use our training systems through Learning System Access. This service requires a valid subscription to SAP Learning Hub, professional edition or solution edition for Finance.

  • The SAP Learning Class part takes you to related classroom offering and all scheduled classroom events.

  • Live Sessions are offered as premium content on top of a learning journey. The learning journey directly takes you to the related schedule. You can register for a session after you log on to SAP Learning Hub, professional edition or solution edition for Finance and then exclusively listen to the speaker and address your questions. In some exceptional cases, sessions are recorded. Please check the comments to this blog to see which live sessions are offered next month. 

  • Join our SAP Learning Group moderated by an SAP Learning expert. Ask your questions about digital learning journeys, prepare thoroughly for SAP Certification exams, and work together with other learners to reach your learning goals.

entry.title: Re: SAP CAL S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, Fully-Activated Appliance
entry.content:
@jbungay, the appliance can only be deployed on Linux. Edit: there might be a way to get a custom app server running on WIndows (without using the appliance files) but that's pretty much trial&error. If you have a Linux box with HANA running, just add the app server to it as well (performance will not go down significantly). 

Best, Joerg

entry.title: Re: Strange behavior in Decision step for WORKING_DAY in...
entry.content: Perfect, thank you!

entry.title: Re: Change company code from XYZ to ABC
entry.content: Then I would say that there is no magic trick that you can do. You have to follow a sequence where you create a new purchasing organization, reassign master data and roles, and then delete the old purchasing organization. In general, deleting the pr org is not advisable. However, if there is no history of transaction data, it should be possible.

entry.title: Re: Can't find freight order in warehouse
entry.content:
Can you explain in more details where you get the error, that the freight order can't be found? Are you trying to use the Load or Unload Freight Orders App (which is build for WM managed storage locations) also for IM managed storage locations?

With best regards,
Thomas Rösch, SAP SE

entry.title: Re: Quick and effective performance analysis and visualisation
entry.content:
Excellent blog and great contribution. If possible, please update the images as they seem to have been lost.

Thanks!

entry.title: sftp channel error Dangling meta character '?' near index 17 abc_defghi_jkl_??????????_????.unx
entry.content:
Hello,

We are migrating from FTP to SFTP .In current FTP below file name was working fine but in SFTP we get error as below

file name - abc_defghi_jkl_??????????_????.unx

Exception received: java.util.regex.PatternSyntaxException: Dangling meta character '?' near index 17
abc_defghi_jkl_??????????_????.unx

Can someone help with regular expression which needs to be used with SFTP to make this work 

Thanks,
Sahana

entry.title: Re: PS WBS Settings
entry.content: The "project type" is mostly for identification purposes and very little control functions. Please check with your SAP configuration expert to analyze your PS set-up.

entry.title: Re: Change company code from XYZ to ABC
entry.content: Does system allows us to change the company code once it is created even though if we don’t have any transactional data

entry.title: Re: Read BW BEX Query from ABAP using FM RRW3_GET_QUERY_V...
entry.content:
Hi,

Alternative to FM :

Use your query as Infoprovider and get all the data in ADSO. Then you can ready ADSO to get your desire results.

Thanks.

Regards,

 

Anand

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud: Inventory CDS View for SAP BW
entry.content:
Hi @deepak_rai12  and @MKreitlein , Did you find any way to have quantity and value using CDS views? We are implementing inventory management in datasphere but couldn't find standard CDS view for revaluation.

Please help with your findings, how inventory management can be implemented in datasphere using CDS views.

Regards,

Vivek

entry.title: Re: Intraday memo records are not automatically generate.
entry.content: For custom memo record types, define a Z* entry in the configuration activity "Define Memo Record Types " (SSCUI ID 101029).

entry.title: Re: sftp channel error Dangling meta character '?' near index 17 abc_defghi_jkl_??????????_????.unx
entry.content: People would need to know what pattern you are trying to match.

entry.title: Re: How to set role restriction in the Manage Project Demand
entry.content: Hi @beacon1, Can we have a call? I want to see it closely. Regards, George

entry.title: Re: Create a Table with foreign key relationship in Eclipse.
entry.content:
@AbapCatalog.foreignKey.screenCheck : false

Why this is false?

entry.title: PMIS - Planner group analysis additional input parameter
entry.content:
Hi 

Business wants to have additional input parameters in Transaction MCI4 - PMIS _ Planner group analysis app, 
Input parameters are Order type and user status. 

In some SAP notes observed PMIS -planner group analysis report going to update some of table. 

Please suggest on proceeding for enhancement on above parameter will have any impact with future table upgradation.
Also suggest will enhancement logic for input parameter will works for this report. 

Thanks and Regards

Jagadeesh

entry.title: Re: Engineer-to-Order Production with Variant Configurati...
entry.content:
Please refer to SAP Note,

3411622 - How to find details about Engineer-to-Order (ETO)

entry.title: Connexion SAP LBN GTT with SAC possible ?
entry.content:
Hello,

I want to know if we can connect SAP Logistics Business Network Global Track and Trace with SAP Analytics Cloud ?
If yes, can you provide me some details please. 

Thank you,

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements Developer Extensibility Doubt
entry.content: Hi junwu I tried to do it now in my company's environment but the option 'open the SAP UI5 Visual Editor' still does not appear. I am trying to add fields to the 'mdm.cmd.product.maintain' application.

entry.title: Query on running SMIGR_CREATE_DDL in source system
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are planning to perform a heterogeneous system copy for the following environment:

Source System Details:

SAP NetWeaver Release (ECC system):
SAP NETWEAVER 7.5 04
EHP8 FOR SAP ERP 6.0 EHP8 FOR SAP ERP 6.0 03 (08/2016)

DB-SID: DEV
Database: Oracle 19.24.0.0.0
Operating System: AIX 7300-01-02-2320
Hostname: eccdev

Target System Details:
DB-SID: DEV
Database: SAP HANA 2.0 SP79.01
Operating System: SLES 15 SP5
Hostname: eccdev

Kindly provide clarification on my below query.

A) Should i execute the report SHDB_GROUP_TABLES_LOAD_BASED before running the SMIGR_CREATE_DDL report in production client of Soruce system

A) When executing SMIGR_CREATE_DDL under HANA options, should we select the following settings?

1.Table Classification
2.Estimated Table Size
3.SAP Business Suite on HANA

Or should we leave it as default ( 1.Table Classification and 2.Estimated Table Size) and proceed with executing the program? Or do we need to remove all the options when running SMIGR_CREATE_DDL

B)According to the system copy guide, could you please confirm if we need to implement the notes included in the attachment of SAP Note 1908075 (REQUIRED_CORRECTION_NOTES.zip)? My system version is SAP NW 7.5 SPS4 ECC instance.

2279106
2264053
2299268
2312217
2324379
2378341
2421685
2347004
2458360
2471909

When I review the notes, they all refer to SAP BW on HANA or BW/HANA, which caused my confusion. Could you please advise me, as my system is an ECC instance?

Regards
Appuru

entry.title: Cost Center-Based Distribution of SAC Stories to SharePoint or Shared Drive
entry.content:
We are implementing a planning workflow in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) for data preparation, review, and approval. Due to a large number of planners, our client wants to minimize costs and has decided not to use SAC's standard planning workflow.

As a workaround, the client wants to distribute SAC stories based on cost centers to a SharePoint site and a folder on a shared drive. The goal is to automate this process to ensure each planner has access only to their respective cost center’s planning data in both SharePoint and the shared drive.

Key Requirements:

  1. Automated File Generation and Distribution:

    • SAC stories need to be exported (e.g., as Excel/CSV) automatically and distributed to the corresponding SharePoint folders and shared drive locations.

Objectives:

  • Find a cost-effective solution to automate this process.
  • Utilize existing SAC features along with external tools (if necessary) for automation and distribution.

If anyone has experience with similar use cases or recommendations for best practices and tools to achieve this, please share your insights!

entry.title: Re: 401 Unauthorized - Build Apps Unable to Access BTP Destination API
entry.content: Hi Dan , Yes i have both forms participant & IRPA Participant roles assigned. In Postman Trigger also same error.

entry.title: Interface from MQ to SAP ECC (webservices) via BTP IS
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are planning to build interface from MQ to SAP ECC (webservices) using SAP Integration (Cloud Integration). Could you please let me know what should be receiver adapter that should be used to post the data to SAP ECC using webservices URL? Also if someone has already worked on how would be the sample URL look like? because I would need those info to raise FW request, Cloud connector entries .. I mean https://<<host>>:<<port>> ... I mainly interested on port info (is it 8080 or something else) and this would help me before we start developing artifacts..

 

Thanks

Ramu

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud : Pricing in service Contract
entry.content:
Hi @sanketp,

I would recommend following the scope item Service Contract Management (3MO) to execute the scenario as it contains all the apps required, and the product documentation to review the pricing details:

  • Pricing in Service
  • Price Management

Hope this helps.

Arturo Montes de Oca
